name: Pork Chops with Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 6 boneless pork chops, each about 1 inch thick and thawed (6 oz.)
mushrooms, 1 pound (small caps if possible, otherwise large caps quartered)
freshly ground pepper for mushroom sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the pork chops lightly in a skillet with butter and oil over high heat.
2. Season the chops and add white wine or stock. Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
3. In another skillet, sauté mushroom caps with butter until browned. Season with salt, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce.
4. Sprinkle flour over the mushrooms and stir in heavy cream. Continue stirring until lightly thickened.
5. Transfer chops to a hot platter. Remove excess fat from the pan and pour the juices over the mushrooms.
6. Spoon the mushroom sauce over the chops. Serve with fried hominy and salad. Serves 6.
